Your tech update for today

linkedin.title: This Week in Finance & Tech
linkedin.body:
It has been a busy week across the markets and the tech sector. Here is a quick look at the stories that mattered most.

In the tech world, Meta saw its stock price climb on Thursday. Investors seem more confident after the company launched a new AI model and shared progress on its custom chip development. Meanwhile, OpenAI is making waves in the hardware space with a new $230 keyboard designed for its coding app. This move comes as the company faces a lawsuit from Apple, which has accused OpenAI of trade secret theft involving hardware designs.

In the financial markets, SK Hynix is making history. The chip giant raised $26.5 billion in a US share sale. The stock is set to debut on the Nasdaq this Friday, marking the largest ever launch for a foreign company on that exchange.

Elsewhere, we are seeing some interesting business shifts. Chipotle is expanding its footprint by opening its first restaurant in Mexico. This is a notable test for the brand, as many other US fast food chains have found it difficult to succeed in the countries that inspired their menus.

On the international front, Gibraltar is entering a new era. The territory has removed border controls with Spain that had been in place for 118 years. The hope is that this change will boost the local economy by allowing for freer movement.

Finally, a few cautionary notes. The Advertising Standards Authority has warned consumers about portable air conditioners that claim to cool rooms in 90 seconds. The agency says these claims are misleading. Also, there was a frightening incident on a Ryanair flight where a passenger had to be held by his wife for several minutes after a window issue occurred mid-air.
linkedin.cta: Get the full picture on the stories that matter to you at HEADLINNE.com.

instagram.title: Top 5 Finance Stories Today

slides.headline: Chipotle heads to Mexico
slides.explanation: The popular U.S. burrito chain is expanding its reach by opening its very first location in Mexico.

slides.headline: GE stock dips despite better outlook
slides.explanation: GE Aerospace raised its profit forecast but investors were disappointed by slower growth in new orders. The stock price fell as a result.

slides.headline: Short sellers target SpaceX
slides.explanation: Investors are betting against SpaceX as its stock price dips below its IPO level. About 29 percent of available shares are now being shorted.

slides.headline: Tim Draper on the next big bets
slides.explanation: The famous venture capitalist is sharing his thoughts on the current AI market and the big opportunities he regrets missing.

slides.headline: Alphabet shares slide amid AI concerns
slides.explanation: Google parent Alphabet saw its stock drop over 4 percent. Investors worry the company is falling behind in the competitive AI race.

instagram.caption: It is a busy day in the markets with tech giants and global brands making headlines. From SpaceX short sellers to Chipotle's big expansion, we have the latest updates you need to know. Swipe through for the full rundown. Read more on HEADLINNE.com.

instagram.title: Top 5 Things In Tech Today

slides.headline: TikTok under investigation for child safety
slides.explanation: Regulators in the UK are looking into how TikTok verifies the age of its younger users. This follows a review earlier this year that found the app was not doing enough to keep children safe.

slides.headline: AI characters are coming to Fortnite
slides.explanation: Epic Games is introducing 36 new AI-powered characters to Fortnite. Creators will be able to use these NPCs, which have their own consistent voices and personalities, starting on July 30th.

slides.headline: EU requires Google to open up Android data
slides.explanation: New rules in the European Union will force Google to share search data and open up its AI features on Android. Google has warned that these changes could create risks for user privacy and security.

slides.headline: Netflix used AI on 300 shows
slides.explanation: Netflix revealed that generative AI was used in about 300 of its titles. Most of this work happened during the post-production phase to help improve quality and speed up the process.

slides.headline: Trump teleprompter operator accused of betting scam
slides.explanation: Federal investigators claim Donald Trump's longtime teleprompter operator used inside information to place bets on Kalshi. The operator allegedly bet on what the former president would say during his speeches.

instagram.caption: From AI characters in your favourite games to new rules for Google in Europe, there is a lot happening in tech today. We have rounded up the biggest stories you need to know. Swipe through for your quick daily update. Read more on HEADLINNE.com.
